seo.title: Building Voice Agents with Vercel AI SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with Vercel AI SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building voice agents with Vercel AI SDK enables real-time conversational AI through speech-to-text, LLM reasoning, and text-to-speech pipelines. Guardrails are essential due to high-stakes risks like hallucinations in streaming responses, unvalidated tool calls, and latency failures that break sub-second user experience. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure response safety, coherence, and performance in production voice deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with Vercel AI SDK
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents process audio streams via WebSocket or edge functions, converting speech-to-text with providers like Deepgram or OpenAI Whisper. The transcribed text feeds into Vercel AI SDK's generateText or streamText for LLM reasoning, often with tool calls for actions like database queries or API integrations. Responses are validated and converted to speech via TTS (e.g., ElevenLabs) before streaming back, all within strict latency budgets.

The pipeline demands sub-second end-to-end latency, making edge deployment critical. Vercel AI SDK's streaming UI components handle partial responses, but without verification, hallucinations or unsafe content can propagate directly to users. Vex middleware intercepts at the generateText/streamText layer to enforce safety and performance SLAs.

Common patterns use React Server Components for server-side rendering of streaming UIs, with Vercel Edge Functions handling the core agent logic.
architectureOverview.diagram:
Client Mic → [STT: Deepgram/OpenAI] → [Vercel AI SDK: generateText/streamText + Tools] → [Vex Middleware: Verify] → [TTS: ElevenLabs] → Client Speaker
                          ↓
                     [Edge Function: <1s Latency]

risks.name: Streaming responses with partial hallucinations
risks.description: Partial LLM tokens can contain misleading or harmful content before full response coherence
risks.mitigation: Vex real-time token verification blocks unsafe partial streams

risks.name: Tool call results not validated before display
risks.description: Unverified tool outputs displayed via TTS can leak sensitive data or execute unsafe actions
risks.mitigation: Vex tool result schema validation + PII redaction

risks.name: Inconsistent structured output across providers
risks.description: Different LLM providers return varying tool call formats breaking voice agent flows

risks.name: Edge function timeouts on complex agent tasks
risks.description: Long-running tool calls exceed Vercel edge limits, dropping conversations
